summary: User policy for File Explorer search suggestions. Microsoft documents the same user policy path, value name, default, and behavior that the app writes.

setting.name: File Explorer Search Box Suggestions
setting.casual_explanation: File Explorer can show suggestion pop-ups as you type into its search box, based on past search entries. This policy decides whether those suggestions are available.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to stop Windows from storing and reusing search-box entries, or to keep File Explorer search quieter and less history-driven.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off reduces stored search history and suggestion pop-ups, but it also removes a convenience feature for repeated searches.
setting.professional_notes: ADMX_WindowsExplorer documents DisableSearchBoxSuggestions. WindowsExplorer.admx maps the user policy to HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\DisableSearchBoxSuggestions with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the File Explorer search-suggestions policy.

allowed_values.label: Search suggestions allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and File Explorer can show recent-query suggestions.

allowed_values.label: Search suggestions disabled
allowed_values.meaning: File Explorer won't show suggestion pop-ups or store search-box entries for future references.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableSearchBoxSuggestions = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 2004 with KB5005101 and newer supported systems, plus Windows 11

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the policy baseline and does not imply that the user has never typed searches before.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who like repeated search suggestions
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want File Explorer to keep ordinary recent-query suggestion behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to stop the suggestion pop-ups and search-history storage.
tradeoffs: No policy-based privacy restriction is enforced.

recommended_profiles.label: Privacy focused
avoid_for: Users who depend on recent-query suggestions for convenience

states.rationale: Turn off recent-query suggestions and related search-box storage.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want fewer search pop-ups and less stored search-box history.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want File Explorer to help recall previous search entries.
tradeoffs: Repeated searches may be slightly less convenient.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: DisableSearchBoxSuggestions
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the user policy that turns off recent-query suggestions in the File Explorer search box.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsExplorer.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DisableSearchBoxSuggestions with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the Explorer policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableSearchBoxSuggestions = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DisableSearchBoxSuggestions policy
evidence.summary: WindowsExplorer.admx shows DisableSearchBoxSuggestions under HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy stops File Explorer from suggesting recent search queries and prevents search-box entries from being stored in the registry; disabling or not configuring it leaves suggestion pop-ups and storage enabled. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: Microsoft documents DisableSearchBoxSuggestions directly in the ADMX_WindowsExplorer Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\DisableSearchBoxSuggestions. The same page describes that enabling the policy disables recent-query suggestions and prevents search-box entries from being stored, and the app writes that exact user policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Disables suggesting recent queries for the Search Box and prevents entries into the Search Box from being stored in the registry for future references. ... If you enable this policy, File Explorer won't show suggestion pop-ups as users type into the Search Box, and it won't store Search Box entries into the registry for future references. ADMX mapping: Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer; Registry Value Name DisableSearchBoxSuggestions.
validation_proof.notes: The ADMX_WindowsExplorer Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact user policy path and value name the app writes, and its policy description matches the disabled behavior. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-disablesearchboxsuggestions-admx.
